info.title: Application Launchpad API
info.description:
Application Launchpad is a Sealos service for deploying and managing containerized applications on Kubernetes. This API allows you to create, query, update, delete, start, pause, and restart applications programmatically.

## Authentication

All requests require a valid kubeconfig passed in the `Authorization` header.

Encode the kubeconfig YAML string with `encodeURIComponent()` before setting the header:

```
Authorization: <encodeURIComponent(kubeconfigYaml)>
```

Obtain your kubeconfig from the Sealos console user menu. A missing or invalid kubeconfig results in a `401 Unauthorized` response.

## Errors

All error responses use a unified format:

```json
{
  "error": {
    "type": "validation_error",
    "code": "INVALID_PARAMETER",
    "message": "Request body validation failed.",
    "details": [{ "field": "image.imageName", "message": "Required" }]
  }
}
```

- `type` — high-level category (e.g. `validation_error`, `resource_error`, `internal_error`)
- `code` — stable identifier for programmatic handling
- `message` — human-readable explanation
- `details` — optional extra context; shape varies by `code` (field list, string, or object)

## Operations

**Query** (read-only): returns `200 OK` with data in the response body.

**Mutation** (write): Create → `201 Created` with the created resource. Update/Delete → `204 No Content`.

baseUrl.description: Base URL of your instance (e.g. https://applaunchpad.192.168.x.x.nip.io)

tags.name: Query
tags.description: Read-only operations. Success: `200 OK` with data in the response body.

tags.name: Mutation
tags.description: Write operations. Create: `201 Created` with the new resource. Update/Delete: `204 No Content`.

replicas.description: Number of pod replicas (for fixed instances). Cannot be used with hpa.

hpa.description: Horizontal Pod Autoscaler configuration. If present, enables elastic scaling; if absent, uses fixed replicas.

get.description:
Retrieves complete application configuration and current runtime status.

The response includes all fields needed to inspect or reproduce the application: image, resource quota, ports (with private and public addresses), environment variables, ConfigMap, storage, and current status.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Application name (Kubernetes resource name, must be a valid DNS subdomain: lowercase alphanumeric, hyphens)

replicas.description: Number of pod replicas (used for fixed instances). To switch from HPA to fixed replicas, set this field and omit hpa field.

hpa.description: Horizontal Pod Autoscaler configuration. To switch from fixed replicas to HPA, set this field and omit replicas field.

quota.description: Quota configuration including HPA. For mode switching: provide either replicas (fixed) or hpa (elastic), not both.

args.description: Container command arguments (each element is a separate arg)

imageRegistry.description: Image pull secret configuration. Set to null to switch to public image.

image.description: Container image configuration. Set imageRegistry to null to switch to public image.

valueFrom.description: Reference to secret for the value

configMap.description: ConfigMap configurations (COMPLETE REPLACEMENT). Pass all ConfigMap entries to keep. Empty array removes all ConfigMaps. Omit field to keep existing ConfigMaps unchanged.

items.description: Simplified storage configuration (name auto-generated from path)

storage.description: Storage configurations (COMPLETE REPLACEMENT). Pass all storage entries to keep. Empty array removes all storage. Omit field to keep existing storage unchanged. Name is auto-generated from path.

number.description: Port number (1-65535) - required for new ports, optional for updates

protocol.description: Network protocol. http/grpc/ws enable public domain access, tcp/udp/sctp enable NodePort

isPublic.description: Whether to expose this port via public domain (only effective for http/grpc/ws protocols)

portName.description: Port name (include this to update existing port, omit to create new port)

items.description: Port configuration. Include portName to update existing port. Omit portName to create new port. Ports not included in the array will be deleted.

ports.description: Port configurations (COMPLETE REPLACEMENT). Include portName to update existing port, omit portName to create new port. Ports not included will be deleted. Pass empty array [] to remove all ports.

schema.title: Update Application Configuration
schema.description: Schema for updating application configuration. IMPORTANT: configMap, storage, and ports are COMPLETE REPLACEMENTS when provided - pass all items you want to keep. Use empty array [] to remove all items.

delete.description:
Permanently deletes the application and all associated Kubernetes resources (Deployments/StatefulSets, Services, Ingresses, ConfigMaps, Secrets).

Key points:
- Pods are terminated gracefully (30s grace period)
- For StatefulSet applications, PVCs are preserved by default
- Idempotent: returns `204` even if the application does not exist
- **Irreversible**

post.description:
Resumes a paused application by restoring replica count or HPA configuration.

Pods are restored to handle traffic. Services and ingresses remain active throughout. Typically takes 1–3 minutes to reach full availability. If the application is already running, returns `204` immediately (idempotent).

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Application name (Kubernetes resource name, must be a valid DNS subdomain: lowercase alphanumeric, hyphens)

post.description:
Temporarily stops an application by scaling replicas to zero while preserving its configuration.

Key points:
- Replica/HPA state is stored and restored on next start
- Pods are terminated gracefully (30s grace period)
- Services and ingresses remain but route no traffic
- Storage is preserved
- Typical compute cost reduction: 60–80%
- This operation is idempotent: pausing an already-paused application returns `204` with no changes

patch.description:
Incrementally updates storage volumes for a StatefulSet application.

Key points:
- Only StatefulSet applications are supported (Deployment returns `400`)
- Incremental merge: pass only the volumes you want to add or resize — unlisted volumes are preserved
- To remove a volume, use `PATCH /apps/{name}` with a complete storage replacement
- Storage can only be expanded, not shrunk (PVC limitation)
- Changes are applied by deleting and recreating the StatefulSet — brief downtime is expected

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Application name (Kubernetes resource name, must be a valid DNS subdomain: lowercase alphanumeric, hyphens)

items.description: Simplified storage configuration (name auto-generated from path)

storage.description: Storage configurations to update (incremental). Only includes storage to add or modify, existing storage not listed will be preserved. Name is auto-generated from path.

post.description:
Performs a rolling restart of all application pods while maintaining service availability.

Key points:
- Zero-downtime for applications with multiple replicas; 30–90s downtime for single-replica applications
- Typical duration: 30–90s (single replica) to 2–5 min (multiple replicas)
- Does **not** update configuration — use `PATCH /apps/{name}` for config changes
